title: The Moms

jokes.text: Could I get your lucretia mott on this?
jokes.setup: Character asks for signature on paperwork
jokes.punchline: Uses obscure historical figure's name as pun for 'John Hancock'
jokes.explanation: Clever historical wordplay that requires knowledge of both Lucretia Mott and John Hancock. Very 30 Rock brand of intellectual comedy.

jokes.text: after their ill-fated decision to re-air the pilot of bitch hunter
jokes.setup: Explaining why they need to do Mother's Day show
jokes.punchline: References fictional show 'Bitch Hunter' as NBC programming failure
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock absurdist callback that mocks NBC programming decisions

jokes.text: Put the mimosas down! Bitch.
jokes.setup: Previous reference to 'Bitch Hunter'
jokes.punchline: Delivers actual Bitch Hunter dialogue
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - delivers the actual content of the ridiculous show just referenced

jokes.text: Necktie. Too late, Jackie.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to order flowers for Mother's Day
jokes.punchline: His mother dismisses his attempt as pathetically inadequate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen character moment - dismissive, cutting, and shows Jack's eternal inadequacy with her

jokes.text: It's little Frankie in the bathtub, getting ready for the senior prom
jokes.setup: Mother offering a photo for the wall
jokes.punchline: Suggests inappropriate bathtub photo from prom night
jokes.explanation: Perfect embarrassing mother moment with absurd detail about bathtub/prom combination

jokes.text: You remember that when a man buys you an expensive meal.
jokes.setup: Mother explaining nothing is free
jokes.punchline: Gives outdated advice about male expectations
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing generational divide and mother's old-fashioned views

jokes.text: Yeah, that's happening a lot.
jokes.setup: Mother's advice about men buying expensive meals
jokes.punchline: Liz sarcastically indicates this never happens to her
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz self-deprecating response with perfect timing

jokes.text: I sexually assaulted scottie pippen in 1997.
jokes.setup: Denying being in Chicago and meeting someone
jokes.punchline: Admits to completely different inappropriate behavior there
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - character denies innocent thing but admits to outrageous criminal act. Perfect 30 Rock absurdity.

jokes.text: Her name might be Cheryl, and she was wearing a red shirt in 1984.
jokes.setup: Tracy's information about finding his mother
jokes.punchline: Hilariously vague and useless identifying information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character moment - grandiose personality with completely useless actual information

jokes.text: Like when they was looking for John McCain's running mate.
jokes.setup: Wanting to see list of actresses to play his mother
jokes.punchline: Compares it to VP selection process
jokes.explanation: Topical reference that works for Tracy's ego but feels a bit dated/forced

jokes.text: I'm kidding. This needs to be taken seriously.
jokes.setup: Previous joke about McCain VP process
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself about seriousness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tracy's inconsistency and self-importance

jokes.text: our lady of reluctant integration in waltham
jokes.setup: Describing a church where bingo is played
jokes.punchline: Church name references forced racial integration
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly crafted fake church name that perfectly captures Colleen's casual bigotry and the show's dark wit

jokes.text: who pours scotch like a woman
jokes.setup: Describing Jack through gossip chain
jokes.punchline: Insults his masculinity through alcohol-pouring technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen insult that's both specific and emasculating in her particular way

jokes.text: ♪ Oh, the fiddle's in the creek ♪ ♪ and the frog's in the kitchen ♪ I apologize, ma'am. That is not a song.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to entertain Colleen with song
jokes.punchline: Sings nonsensical lyrics then immediately admits it's not real
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - tries to be helpful but is completely incompetent in the most Kenneth way possible

jokes.text: You make me very nervous. Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Colleen's intimidating presence
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's honest admission of terror
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Kenneth's anxiety and Colleen's intimidating nature

jokes.text: What? Thanks a lot, lutz.
jokes.setup: Lutz assumes someone is adopted based on their Asian mother
jokes.punchline: Character's annoyed reaction to racist assumption
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe moment about casual racism, gets laugh from discomfort but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Wrong tree, mom. Gotcha.
jokes.setup: Mother flirting with wardrobe guy Lee
jokes.punchline: Character corrects her about Lee being gay
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom joke about mother misreading gay man as potential romantic interest

jokes.text: I made us both rompers to wear.
jokes.setup: Mother brought clothes for TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Reveals she made matching rompers
jokes.explanation: Good embarrassing mother moment with specific absurd detail about adult rompers

jokes.text: My...Friend has to go strangle her anxiety pillow.
jokes.setup: Making excuse for someone leaving
jokes.punchline: Specific weird detail about strangling anxiety pillow
jokes.explanation: Very 30 Rock specific absurd detail that feels both neurotic and oddly believable

jokes.text: It's bad luck for a married woman to be a bridesmaid. It brings fever and disease.
jokes.setup: Discussing bridesmaid dress
jokes.punchline: Bizarre superstition about fever and disease
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist mother superstition that escalates from bad luck to medical consequences

jokes.text: How do you get credit at a mattress store?
jokes.setup: Learning Liz isn't even common law married
jokes.punchline: Asks about specific practical implication
jokes.explanation: Funny practical question that shows mother's different generational priorities

jokes.text: That's what feminism does. It makes smart girls with nice birthing shapes believe in fairytales.
jokes.setup: Liz saying she hasn't met 'the one'
jokes.punchline: Blames feminism with crude biological description
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about anti-feminist sentiment with hilariously clinical 'birthing shapes' phrase

jokes.text: I'm waiting for astronaut Mike Dexter.
jokes.setup: Defending herself against settling
jokes.punchline: Creates specific impossible romantic ideal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - specific nerdy fantasy with astronaut profession and generic hot guy name

jokes.text: Who turns out to be the secret king of Monaco.
jokes.setup: Already described impossible astronaut Mike Dexter
jokes.punchline: Adds even more impossible royal twist
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that shows Liz's impossible romantic standards getting even more ridiculous

jokes.text: Greener banana
jokes.setup: Explaining why John is single
jokes.punchline: Uses fruit metaphor for younger woman
jokes.explanation: Decent metaphor for age preferences in dating, fits the crude mother character

jokes.text: It's not an implant. It's just a little yank up, you know? I mean, I got the meat, Jack.
jokes.setup: Discussing breast enhancement surgery
jokes.punchline: Crude description of procedure using 'meat' metaphor
jokes.explanation: Appropriately cringe character moment that establishes Verna's crudeness

jokes.text: Like a cantaloupe and a ziploc bag of mushroom soup.
jokes.setup: Verna asking Jack to feel the difference in her breasts
jokes.punchline: Jack's disgusted comparison to disparate textures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant and disgusting visual comparison that perfectly captures the grotesque situation

jokes.text: Oh, wait, is she aquaman's girlfriend?
jokes.setup: Being told about actress Novella Nelson
jokes.punchline: Confuses her with comic book character
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character moment showing his pop culture confusion and self-centeredness

jokes.text: That could be anyone! We all look the same to me.
jokes.setup: Being told actress looks like him
jokes.punchline: Makes racist comment about his own race
jokes.explanation: Clever character moment where Tracy's narcissism leads him to be racist against himself

jokes.text: Ugh, these overalls are chafing. I can't wear these pajamas fishing! Pajamaralls. Pajamaralways.
jokes.setup: Fake commercial for clothing item
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous product name portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock fake product with absurd name that sounds both stupid and believable

jokes.text: I think she's good enough for the star of sherlock homie.
jokes.setup: Defending actress choice for Tracy
jokes.punchline: References Tracy's fake movie with pun title
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Tracy's ridiculous movie projects with racial pun

jokes.text: Two women, Jack, at the same time. What are you, Italian?
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his romantic situation
jokes.punchline: Colleen's ethnic stereotype about infidelity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen response with casual ethnic stereotyping delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I know it's gay, but it's my gay problem, and I'm handling it.
jokes.setup: Explaining his romantic indecision
jokes.punchline: Misuses 'gay' to mean his personal issue
jokes.explanation: Good Jack character moment showing his attempt to be modern while still being inappropriate

jokes.text: Just bring my son one of everything. That way, he doesn't have to choose.
jokes.setup: Jack's indecision between two women
jokes.punchline: Mother enables his indecision with restaurant order
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen moment that shows how she enables Jack's worst tendencies while seeming helpful

jokes.text: In Florida, everybody's always asking me, 'where did you get that? You made it? Holy crap! Where do I commission one?'
jokes.setup: Defending her homemade clothes
jokes.punchline: Obviously fake story about people wanting to buy her crafts
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Verna's delusions about her terrible craft skills

jokes.text: Because of the trampoline accident.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she hasn't worked in 15 years
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd injury explanation
jokes.explanation: Good absurd detail that suggests both adult foolishness and convenient excuse-making

jokes.text: That is quality stitching.
jokes.setup: Verna tears up expensive dress in anger
jokes.punchline: Immediately acknowledges its superior construction
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic beat where character's anger is interrupted by grudging professional respect

jokes.text: Damn the tiny, brown hands that made this.
jokes.setup: Admiring the dress construction
jokes.punchline: Racist comment about garment workers
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about labor practices that fits Verna's character but feels somewhat cheap

jokes.text: A computer is a great big thing with a green screen.
jokes.setup: Mother touching Liz's laptop
jokes.punchline: Describes computers as old mainframes
jokes.explanation: Standard generational gap humor about technology, works for character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I remember someone who got a three on her history ap test.
jokes.setup: Saying Liz's standards should be high
jokes.punchline: References specific academic failure
jokes.explanation: Good mother put-down that shows she remembers Liz's failures in specific detail

jokes.text: while Waldo, the town perv, watched from the bushes.
jokes.setup: Describing losing virginity to Ed
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of voyeuristic witness
jokes.explanation: Great absurd detail that makes intimate moment hilariously inappropriate

jokes.text: I got a job at sterling Cooper.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she couldn't follow Ed
jokes.punchline: References Mad Men workplace
jokes.explanation: Nice TV reference that fits the period but feels somewhat forced into the story

jokes.text: Different times. Octomom.
jokes.setup: Saying she was an old maid at 26
jokes.punchline: Contrasts with modern reference to Octomom
jokes.explanation: Good economy in showing generational change with unexpected modern reference

jokes.text: Not even when I watched ed aldrin walk on the moon.
jokes.setup: Saying she never regretted marrying Dick instead of Ed
jokes.punchline: Reveals Ed became famous astronaut
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that reveals the magnitude of what mother gave up, perfectly timed revelation

jokes.text: Yeah, but I always called him ed, because our town had, like, five buzzes.
jokes.setup: Confirming it's Buzz Aldrin
jokes.punchline: Explains why she used different name
jokes.explanation: Good absurd detail that makes the famous name feel mundane in small town context

jokes.text: Laura linney could have played you in the hbo original movie moon wives!
jokes.setup: Liz processing what mother gave up
jokes.punchline: Creates specific prestige TV movie title
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing how Liz imagines the glamorous life mother missed, specific HBO reference

jokes.text: drive around with one headlight out
jokes.setup: Jack threatening Verna about payment
jokes.punchline: Metaphor for uneven breast surgery
jokes.explanation: Clever visual metaphor that's both specific to the situation and generally applicable

jokes.text: I am a movie star, a television actor, and a guinness book of world record holder for most car accidents in a single year.
jokes.setup: Tracy defending his status to Novella
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly questionable achievements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character moment showing his warped sense of achievement and dangerous lifestyle

jokes.text: whose resume has 'black judge' on it nine times.
jokes.setup: Tracy insulting Novella Nelson
jokes.punchline: Points out repetitive typecasting in her career
jokes.explanation: Good observation about Hollywood typecasting that fits Tracy's arrogance

jokes.text: who recorded an anti-condom psa? I saved a lot of kids from lame sex!
jokes.setup: Novella insulting Tracy's career
jokes.punchline: Tracy defends ridiculous PSA with twisted logic
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Tracy's ability to spin any criticism positively

jokes.text: I can't say Nicaragua.
jokes.setup: Character reading script
jokes.punchline: Admits inability to pronounce country name
jokes.explanation: Simple character flaw moment that's mildly amusing but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Mrs. Donaghy keeps tricking me and running away, but not anymore. Carl, they need you out in the hall. Yes, ma'am!
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining he won't be tricked again
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets tricked and falls for it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth/Colleen dynamic showing his complete gullibility despite stated awareness

jokes.text: because chest-wise you have the measurements of an altar boy.
jokes.setup: Colleen sizing up Avery as competition
jokes.punchline: Specific religious comparison for flat chest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen insult that's both specific and inappropriately religious

jokes.text: I've got to get back to calling Kenneth 'carl' to see if he ever corrects me. Carl! Yes, ma'am?
jokes.setup: Colleen explaining her psychological experiment
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates Kenneth never corrects her
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Colleen's cruel amusement and Kenneth's extreme deference

jokes.text: Or I would have been born Peter aldrin. And I'd wait for a woman with the right stuff.
jokes.setup: Imagining if mother married Buzz Aldrin
jokes.punchline: Uses astronaut reference 'right stuff' for dating standards
jokes.explanation: Good pun that ties space program reference to dating, fits Liz's nerdy character

jokes.text: Now, remember, in this scenario, I'm a man and my father is an astronaut.
jokes.setup: Liz's fantasy about different life
jokes.punchline: Clarifies the gender-swap aspect of her fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's tendency to over-explain her weird fantasies

jokes.text: I saw him last night at Rupert Murdoch's twister party. I mean, uh, regular party.
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning seeing Buzz Aldrin
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals weird party detail
jokes.explanation: Great glimpse into the absurd rich people parties Jack attends

jokes.text: Because you're talking in the ear that I didn't lose a button in.
jokes.setup: Tracy responding to someone
jokes.punchline: References bizarre injury involving button in ear
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy random injury reference that's both specific and inexplicable

jokes.text: Thank God Terry's dead, or we'd be fighting over him again too.
jokes.setup: Jenna complaining about fighting with Verna
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions being grateful for someone's death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment showing her casual selfishness and dark pragmatism

jokes.text: And she is a good actress. I bought those pajamaralls.
jokes.setup: Tracy defending Novella's acting ability
jokes.punchline: References being convinced by her commercial performance
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Tracy's simple criteria for judging acting talent

jokes.text: Sometimes I sing too beautifully.
jokes.setup: Admitting imperfection
jokes.punchline: Claims her 'flaw' is being too talented
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna character moment showing her inability to admit real flaws

jokes.text: Then you skip the part where you yell at me. And then we both move on to the, uh, make-up sex.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to shortcut relationship conflict
jokes.punchline: Proposes skipping straight to resolution and sex
jokes.explanation: Good Jack character moment showing his transactional approach to relationships

jokes.text: That is a swing.
jokes.setup: Jack's proposition to skip to makeup sex
jokes.punchline: Avery's measured response to his audacity
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that acknowledges the audacity without getting angry

jokes.text: But these tactics have worked on stupider women.
jokes.setup: Avery responding to Jack's manipulation
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges his past success while insulting his exes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Avery line showing her intelligence and confidence while insulting Jack's history

jokes.text: I don't believe in barriers, because I always break them.
jokes.setup: Liz apologizing for entering without knocking
jokes.punchline: Astronaut references breaking barriers literally and figuratively
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Buzz that references his achievements while explaining his philosophy

jokes.text: I see you. I see what you're doing. Return to the night. You've no business here.
jokes.setup: Buzz seeming normal and accomplished
jokes.punchline: Suddenly addresses the moon dramatically
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment that reveals Buzz's psychological issues in completely unexpected way

jokes.text: I once woke up in the air and space museum with a revolver in the waistband of my Jean shorts.
jokes.setup: Buzz explaining his troubled past
jokes.punchline: Specific bizarre detail about waking up armed in museum
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific detail that combines the absurd (jean shorts) with the disturbing (revolver)

jokes.text: I own you! You dumb moon! I walked on your face! Don't you know it's day? Idiot!
jokes.setup: Buzz offering to let Liz yell at moon
jokes.punchline: His aggressive, personal attack on the moon
jokes.explanation: Outstanding escalation that's both hilarious and reveals deep psychological issues. Perfect 30 Rock absurdity.

jokes.text: even though it was Margarita Monday at dockside Joe's.
jokes.setup: Verna claiming she sacrificed during Jenna's birth
jokes.punchline: Reveals she missed drink specials, not something important
jokes.explanation: Perfect Verna character moment showing her warped priorities and selfishness

jokes.text: Why does your chest feel weird? Uh, because I love you so much.
jokes.setup: Jenna hugging Verna after reconciliation
jokes.punchline: References the uneven breast surgery
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier plot point that provides physical comedy during emotional moment

jokes.text: I think I'm ready for the sex talk.
jokes.setup: Tracy bonding with fake mother
jokes.punchline: Asks for sex talk like a child despite being adult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tracy's regression to childlike state with mother figure

jokes.text: before my hereditary dementia sets in?
jokes.setup: Mother urging Liz to start family
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions inherited mental illness
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that adds urgency to mother's request while revealing family medical history

jokes.text: I assume herpes.
jokes.setup: Colleen listing Jack's romantic failures
jokes.punchline: Casually assumes he has STDs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen moment - casual cruelty delivered with maternal concern

jokes.text: I'll be circling the globe in my coffin rocket. Something I saw on tv. It's very expensive.
jokes.setup: Colleen talking about her mortality
jokes.punchline: References ridiculous space burial from TV
jokes.explanation: Great absurd detail that shows Colleen's vanity extending beyond death

jokes.text: I can't!
jokes.setup: Jack asking mother to tell him what to do for first time
jokes.punchline: Colleen admits she can't help for first time
jokes.explanation: Perfect emotional payoff showing both characters' growth and mutual vulnerability

jokes.text: Carl would never let you down. Carl, that does not sound right. Kevin--no. Who am I? Is it Keith? No, that's not-- yes, I'm Keith.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to remember his own name
jokes.punchline: Gets progressively more confused about his identity
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing how Colleen's psychological manipulation has worked completely

jokes.text: ♪ Don't go to sleep with a frown in your pocket ♪ ♪ take it to the yard and tie it to a rocket ♪ ♪ shoot it to the moon ♪ ♪ you'll feel better soon ♪
jokes.setup: Mother's Day show ending song
jokes.punchline: Absurdist lyrics about literal frown disposal
jokes.explanation: Good parody of cheesy TV show endings with appropriately absurd children's song logic

tmdb_overview: Liz Lemon meets her mother's old flame, while Jack Donaghy's mother, Colleen, gets tangled in his relationships with Avery Jessup and high school love, Nancy Donovan. Jenna's mother, Verna returns, while Tracy learns a lesson from his "mom".
insight: Mother-centric episode sustains 85-point pace through character escalation, delivering 77 jokes across 40 minutes.
